taxonomy: Tragacantha pycnocephala belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It is a type of shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is further classified as being in the subfamily Faboideae. Within the genus Tragacantha, it is one of the species of plants that are often referred to as needle grass or needle bush. The specific classification is Tragacantha pycnocephala, which is a unique identifier for this particular species.
uses: Tragacantha pycnocephala, also known as the needle grass, is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Middle East and North Africa.
